findings.description: The dogfood loop: phases 3-5 each emitted their own evaluator events through the very machinery they were building, so loom events aggregate reported real metrics (13 spawned, 4 recused, 1 finding) by Phase 5 — the substrate validating itself end-to-end.
Reconcile-as-base-commit cleanly resolved the opening manifest-vs-git drift (Phase 2 merged but manifest stale, reconcile + a handoff stranded on an unpushed branch) without losing prior work.

Incremental decomposition under discovery: Phase 3 began as one type-change and became D1/D2a/D2b-1/D2b-2 as real gaps surfaced (no skill-callable append primitive; recusal had no first-class signal), each a clean reviewable unit.

The PLAN consistently understated reality: Phase 3 split into 4 units it never named (the append verb, the recusal convention); Phase 4 cited an unsupported --since=30d form + JSONL-era "drop trailing partials" language; Phase 5 provenance named a "session id" the CLI had no source for. The PLAN lagged a substrate that moved between planning and execution.

Evaluator panel infrastructure friction on every panel: derive-panel emitted a stale agent name (evaluator-react-api, renamed to evaluator-react) requiring manual remap, and evaluators twice exhausted their tool budget without emitting a verdict until re-prompted with a stricter packet.
findings.evidence: checkin 10 corrections; the Phase 4 panel rerun

findings.description: parse-and-aggregate blind spots: recusal needed a whole new convention (D2b-1) because "approved + note" was structurally invisible to aggregation, and it silently drops prose ADVISORY notes from approved verdicts (the project's only real finding-emitted surfaced only by deviating from the emit spec).

Bake the budget-discipline framing ("do AT MOST ONE spot-check, trust the pre-computed verification, emit VERDICT now") into the canonical dense-packet template — it reliably fixed evaluator budget-exhaustion across three panels where the soft "emit parse-failure on running out" fallback failed.
derive-panel must resync with the live agent registry (or the loop must map its output to current names) — stale agent names on every panel is a standing tax, not a one-off.

Verify each unit's premise against current source at contract time rather than trusting the PLAN — the units that re-grounded before drafting went smoothest; the substrate (binaries, codegen'd agents, conventions) moves under a stale plan.
findings.evidence: PLAN-lag corrections, checkins 07/10/11; cached-binary lag forced node-source invocation all session

findings.description: parse-and-aggregate should surface approved-verdict prose ADVISORY notes (or require evaluators to flag-to-advise); today an approved-with-advisory evaluator loses its advice entirely.

Bake VERDICT: recused into the codegen evaluator verdict-format fragment + recompile the fleet + run the live-spawn smoke; the ev-loop dense packet only bridges the recusal instruction at runtime today.

Bring the stale events-all-types.jsonl fixture + the EVENT_NAMES set in types.test.ts current to the full Event union — they are a core-13 snapshot missing ~20 members (research-*, plan-*, scope-shift, rpi-*, auto-mode-*, evaluator-*).

An end-marker variant for research append blocks — the chosen heading+provenance-comment format has no terminator, so block boundaries are the next ## / --- and appended prose must avoid those. Also open: route loom-research + ev-loop skill-side emits through loom events append, and add a tsc gate to CI (npm test is vitest-only, so type guarantees are unenforced).
